ð° GAYâS CREAMERY, DAWLISH ð°

Celebrating their one hundredth birthday in business this year is the wildly popular Gayâs Creamery in Dawlish. 

Serving a wide variety of cakes, pasties, Cheesy Steves, cream teas and their ever popular ice cream with a big dollop of clotted cream on top! 

To celebrate their centenary, this beautiful vintage Bedford van is sitting outside the shop and it looks magnificent. Have you been to see it yet? 

Dawlish is full of wonderful independent family-run businesses, itâs what makes this town so very special. So make sure you come to Dawlish soon and enjoy the unique shopping experience and a plethora of tasty food! 

Fancy holidaying in Dawlish next year? Take a look at Pilchard Cottage here ð http://www.sykescottages.co.uk/cottage/Devon-Dawlish/Pilchard-Cottage-1143949.html

#chef #food #comedy #relatable #healthy

Keith Floyd introducing traditional Black Country cooking and meeting the f***** king. 

In the U.K. the word f****t refers to a small meatball.

In general, traditional f*g*ots are made from minced pork offal, such as liver and heart, mixed with herbs and breadcrumbs, then shaped into balls and baked. They are often served with mushy peas and onion gravy.

Richard is an absolute gem. He completely fails in the task he is set ð¤£. He also visibly shifts back in discomfort at being referred to as the âfagg*t kingâ as I think we all would.

messages.content: Very much the way it reads, the Ã¤ is Ahh, so Kalsarik-ahh-nit with a partially silent T

share.share_text:
What a week, maybe we have something for you... Although it's dark and freezing cold in Finland for almost half the year, the people there are among the happiest in the world. First place in the World Happiness Report seven times in a row - what is their secret? 

Perhaps it's the high quality of life, the endless forests or the many saunas. But certainly also because of KalsarikÃ¤nnit. Sounds complicated, but means something like: âGetting drunk at home in your knickersâ. 

A word that perfectly summarises how you can experience maximum enjoyment with minimum effort - alone, relaxed, no frills. No wonder this philosophy has so much to do with Finland's relaxed attitude to life. Instead of the eternal pursuit of perfection, Finns indulge in pleasure, irrationality and a little intoxication. 

Perhaps this is precisely the antithesis of our optimisation mania: simply letting go and celebrating life. Is KalsarikÃ¤nnit perhaps something for you?
